summary: Large Character Select Screen decompilation PR focused on src/melee/mn/mncharsel. It added several thousand lines of C for mnCharSel behavior, improved or matched multiple functions, updated public prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed signatures, and refined CSS-related static globals and structs. The decomp.dev bot reported +2904 matched bytes, 3 new full matches, and 10 improved unmatched items; there was no substantive human review text in the slice.
what_changed: Added large C implementations/partial implementations in mncharsel.c, including mnCharSel_8025DB34, fn_8025F0E0, mnCharSel_8025FB50, mnCharSel_8025FDEC, mnCharSel_CursorThink, fn_80262648, fn_80262F44, and fn_802633B0.
Brought mnCharSel_8025D5AC and mnCharSel_8025EE8C closer to or fully matching, including local-variable/codegen reshaping around lb_80011E24 and HSD_ForeachAnim call sequences.
Added a local CSSCursorData struct and static cursor/model arrays in mncharsel.c; also introduced a CSSCharModel struct in the same area of the diff.

Updated mncharsel.h prototypes from unknown signatures to typed forms such as void fn_8025F0E0(HSD_GObj*), void mnCharSel_8025FB50(u8 door, s32 arg1), s32 mnCharSel_8025FDEC(u8 door), void fn_80262648(HSD_GObj*), void fn_80262F44(HSD_GObj*), and void fn_802633B0(HSD_GObj*).

Added static/global declarations in mncharsel.static.h for data tables mnCharSel_804D50C8, mnCharSel_804D50CC, mnCharSel_804D50D0, mnCharSel_804D50D8, mnCharSel_804D50E0, plus mnCharSel_804D6CCC, mnCharSel_804D6CF3, and mnCharSel_804D6CF7.
Adjusted CSSDoorsData in types.h by replacing int xcf/xd3 with u8 scroll_flag and float xcf/xd3, matching observed scroll/page position behavior in the new code.
Expanded CSSDoorsData2 with u8 stocks, xf6, and xf7 fields, and updated static initializers accordingly.
Added includes needed by the decompiled code, including MSL/trigf.h, baselib controller/dobj/mobj/gobjgxlink/random headers, gm_1601.h, and lb_00B0.h.
smart_moves: Used the decomp.dev bot's matching report to validate measurable progress: fn_80262F44 reached 100%, mnCharSel_8025EE8C reached 100%, mnCharSel_8025D5AC reached 100%, and several large functions moved from 0% to substantial partial matches.

Preserved address-based names for still-unknown functions while upgrading signatures where the parameter/return types were supported by call sites, improving compile-time knowledge without inventing semantic names.

Modeled CSS state more accurately in types.h: scroll/page fields are floats in the new code paths, and the added scroll_flag separates byte state from float positions instead of forcing all fields through int placeholders.
Reused consistent HSD animation idioms: lb_80011E24 to fetch a JObj by joint index, HSD_ForeachAnim with JOBJ_TYPE and TOBJ_MASK/MOBJ_MASK/ALL_TYPE_MASK, HSD_JObjAnimAll, then HSD_AObjStopAnim.

Applied matching-oriented compiler controls and code-shape anchors such as #pragma dont_inline around mnCharSel_8025DAA0, #pragma auto_inline around mnCharSel_8025FDEC, PAD_STACK, explicit u8/s8/f32 casts, and temporary locals to steer register/stack allocation.
decomp_lessons: For large menu-state functions, recover the surrounding struct layout in parallel with function bodies; the xcf/xd3 float correction in CSSDoorsData explains new scrolling math in fn_8025F0E0.
When a function operates mostly on HSD_JObj animation state, matching often depends on preserving exact call order and masks, not just high-level behavior.
Typed prototypes should be advanced opportunistically as functions are decompiled; replacing UNK_PARAMS with HSD_GObj*, u8, s32, or s32 return values makes later callers easier to match.
Address-style names remain acceptable for unknown mnCharSel functions, but parameter names like door, idx, input, and gobj are useful when directly evident from use.

Large CSS functions mix gameplay data and UI animation state: match_type, number of doors, p_kind, p_kind_prev, costume, team, handicap, CPU level, tag text visibility, and audio cues all interact in one update path.
naming_conventions: Keep module prefix mnCharSel_ for known-address functions in mncharsel until a stronger semantic name is available.
Use fn_802xxxxx for anonymous functions where no better semantic name is evidenced, while still giving typed signatures.
Use local names like door, idx, gobj, input, sel_icon, hud_idx, costume, p_kind, p_kind_prev, team, and cpu_level when usage is clear from existing CSS data structures.
Structs discovered for the character select screen use CSS-prefixed names such as CSSCursorData, CSSCharModel, CSSDoorsData, and CSSDoorsData2.
Static/global symbols retain address names such as mnCharSel_804D6CB0 and mnCharSel_803F0DFC until the role is proven.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: #pragma dont_inline on/off was added around mnCharSel_8025DAA0, indicating inlining behavior mattered for neighboring matches.
#pragma auto_inline off/on was added around mnCharSel_8025FDEC, likely to control compiler inlining decisions for matching.
PAD_STACK(0x40) and PAD_STACK(40) appear in newly decompiled functions to force stack frame layout.
Temporary locals and expression shaping were used in mnCharSel_8025D5AC, including assigning new_var2 = &sp54 inside a call and using new_var = sp50 before HSD_ForeachAnim.
A no-op empty conditional on sp54 was inserted in mnCharSel_8025D5AC, which is a classic decomp codegen shim; its purpose is not explained in comments but the bot reports the function reached 100%.
Explicit casts such as (u8), (s8)(u8), (int), and (f32) are common in the diff and should be preserved where they affect compare/load/convert codegen.
Repeated HSD_ForeachAnim/HSD_JObjAnimAll/HSD_AObjStopAnim sequences are written out rather than abstracted, preserving instruction order for matching.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The only issue comment was the decomp.dev bot report: matched code increased to 61.40% (+0.07%, +2904 bytes).
Bot-reported new full matches: fn_80262F44 (+1040 bytes), mnCharSel_8025EE8C (+75 bytes), and mnCharSel_8025D5AC (+5 bytes).
Bot-reported large partial improvements included mnCharSel_CursorThink to 89.29%, mnCharSel_8025DB34 to 87.80%, fn_802633B0 to 87.66%, fn_8025F0E0 to 93.36%, and fn_80262648 to 96.72%.
follow_up_queries: Inspect later PRs touching mnCharSel_CursorThink to see how the remaining 10.71% was closed.
Search for follow-up renames of fn_8025F0E0, fn_80262648, fn_80262F44, and fn_802633B0 once semantic behavior became clearer.
Check subsequent changes to CSSDoorsData and CSSDoorsData2 to confirm whether xcf/xd3/scroll_flag/stocks field names were refined.
Look for removal or cleanup of matching shims in mnCharSel_8025D5AC after adjacent functions stabilized.
Compare later decomp.dev reports for main/melee/mn/mncharsel to track whether the large partial functions reached 100%.
